mechanism_narrative: A flat or falling FEDFUNDS rate keeps discount rates compressed, so equity valuations expand and the S&P 500 posts positive forward returns over the next 20 trading days. The signal is strongest when inflation is not accelerating materially, because stable CPIAUCSL reduces the odds of imminent tightening.
failure_condition: Reject if periods with flat or declining FEDFUNDS do not show higher 20d SPX returns than the unconditional sample, or if the sign flips and returns are systematically negative when CPIAUCSL inflation is stable.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent baseline signals labor-market deterioration, weaker household income growth, and slower earnings expectations, which should translate into negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The effect is most informative when PAYEMS is also soft, because weak payroll growth confirms the unemployment shock.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.2 percentage points over 2 months
failure_condition: Reject if unemployment-triggered windows do not produce below-baseline 20d SPX returns, or if the effect disappears when PAYEMS is also declining, indicating no labor-market confirmation.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized improvement in RSAFS and HOUST indicates stronger consumer spending and housing demand, supporting revenue growth expectations and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. This mechanism requires both demand proxies to turn up together because either series alone can reflect noise or transitory revision effects.
failure_condition: Reject if joint upturns in RSAFS and HOUST fail to outperform the unconditional 60d return distribution, or if only one of the two series improves while SPX returns remain unchanged.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in INDPRO while CPIAUCSL remains contained implies stronger real activity without an inflation surprise, which reduces recession risk and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism fails when production rises alongside a clear inflation breakout, because then markets can price tighter policy instead of growth.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises by more than 0.5% over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if industrial-production acceleration does not lead to higher 20d SPX returns when CPIAUCSL is stable, or if the same setup produces negative returns during repeated test windows.

mechanism_narrative: A negative GDP growth reading in GDPC1 signals broad demand weakness and rising recession probability, which should produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The effect is strongest when RSAFS is also slowing, because weak sales confirm that the output decline is demand-driven rather than inventory noise.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth falls below 0% annualized in the latest quarter
failure_condition: Reject if negative GDPC1 growth does not predict below-baseline 60d SPX returns, or if the effect persists even when RSAFS is accelerating, implying GDP weakness is not being priced as demand deterioration.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in FEDFUNDS after a prolonged low-rate period tightens financial conditions and compresses equity multiples, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days. The signal is only active when CPIAUCSL is not falling, because disinflation would weaken the justification for further tightening.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises by more than 0.25 percentage points over 1 month
failure_condition: Reject if rapid FEDFUNDS increases are not followed by weaker 5d SPX returns, or if returns remain positive despite no corresponding stabilization in CPIAUCSL.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll growth in PAYEMS together with a falling UNRATE indicates durable employment momentum, stronger household demand, and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. This is a regime-conditional labor signal that works best in expansionary phases and fails when labor gains are already fully priced or when the unemployment rate is rising despite payroll growth.
failure_condition: Reject if periods with rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE do not produce above-baseline 20d SPX returns, or if the effect disappears once the employment trend is already broadly known.

mechanism_narrative: A collapse in HOUST after prior stability signals demand rollover in interest-sensitive sectors, weaker future consumption, and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The mechanism is strongest when FEDFUNDS is already elevated, because housing weakness then reflects binding financial conditions rather than a benign supply disturbance.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls by more than 10% over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if large HOUST declines do not predict weaker 60d SPX returns when FEDFUNDS is elevated, or if the signal is absent in repeated downturn episodes.
